key_insight: A general-purpose ACE interatomic potential for niobium can reproduce near-DFT accuracy across diverse crystal, defect, and high-pressure conditions.
engineering_relevance: This enables efficient, large-scale simulation of niobium behavior for applications such as dislocation motion, fracture, and other extreme-environment phenomena.
method: Trained an atomic cluster expansion (ACE) potential on thousands of DFT-generated niobium structures.
Covered a wide range of local atomic environments to improve transferability.
Benchmarked against empirical and ML potentials on phonons, high pressure, barriers, and fracture.
evidence_signal: The model was validated on multiple property classes and demonstrated robustness in a near-million-atom molecular dynamics fracture simulation.
watch_item: Even with strong transferability, performance should still be checked for out-of-distribution niobium configurations or conditions not represented in the DFT training set.
paper_title: A general-purpose atomic cluster expansion interatomic potential for niobium
